title: 1 · admissibility
lead: A defun is admitted only if it is syntactically structurally recursive: every recursive call must pass a car/cdr projection of the same formal, in that formal's position. That is a CHECK on the source text, not a termination proof — no measure, no well-founded relation.

examples.title: app — admitted
cells: (defun app (x y) (if (consp x) (cons (car x) (app (cdr x) y)) y))
examples.note: recurses on (cdr x) in x's position

title: 2 · evaluation
lead: An admitted function runs on quoted data, and the printed value is read off a kernel theorem. ACL2 data here is pure S-expression data: an integer inside a list is rejected rather than silently coerced.

examples.title: (app '(a b) '(c))
cells: (defun app (x y) (if (consp x) (cons (car x) (app (cdr x) y)) y))

examples.title: mixed data — REJECTED
examples.note: integers are typed int terms, not sexpr data

title: 3 · ground defthm, and what gets stored
lead: A ground (equal L R) over quoted data, integer literals and car/cdr/cons/consp/equal/+ goes through a reified Derivable_ACL2 certificate plus a machine-checked soundness metatheorem; what is STORED is the transported base-HOL model equation. #show NAME prints it, and says which route proved it. Goals outside that fragment are driven to a bool literal by certified reduction instead — those ride the defun equations as HYPOTHESES, visible to the left of the turnstile.

examples.title: a defun-backed goal, then #show it
cells: (defun app (x y) (if (consp x) (cons (car x) (app (cdr x) y)) y))
examples.note: reduction route: the defun equation rides as a hypothesis

title: 4 · the hard limit: no induction
lead: Every ACL2 staple you actually want — associativity of app, length of an append — is universally quantified, and needs induction. Induction is NOT implemented in this slice. Such goals are rejected outright; nothing is admitted on faith.

examples.title: app associativity — REJECTED
cells: (defun app (x y) (if (consp x) (cons (car x) (app (cdr x) y)) y))
(defthm app-assoc (equal (app (app x y) z) (app x (app y z))))

examples.title: an implies goal — REJECTED
examples.note: free variable x; implies is not a dialect head

title: 5 · books
lead: #book PATH runs a .lisp book of ACL2 events through this session and prints a per-event tally: transported / admitted / skipped / rejected, each with a reason. Nothing is glossed — a book with unprovable events still loads and still reports them as REJECTED. Paths resolve on the SERVER, relative to its working directory and confined to it (no .., no absolute paths).

examples.title: #book mixed
examples.note: one event per tally category, include-book deps included
